name: Bill's Venison or Buffalo Roast
recipeIngredient: Rinse and dry off the roast
Cut slits in the top side of the roast about 2-3 inches apart (adjust based on spice preference)
Alternate half a clove of garlic with slices of green serano or jalapeno peppers in each slit
Turn the roast over, repeat the process
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover the entire surface of the roast heavily with cheap prepared mustard
2. Place the mustard-covered roast in a dry pan
3. Cover the roast heavily with yellow onion rings
4. Put the pan in a preheated oven set to 300°F (°C)
5. Roast until the meat is very tender
6. Slice across the grain and serve with brown rice or wild rice and greens
Note: Buffalo should be cooked at low heat for an extended period to become tender. If cooked at too high a temperature, it will become tough and may never soften again. This recipe was originally Bill's Grandma Johnson's venison roast recipe but has also been successfully used for buffalo.
